hypothesis: If a large language model is augmented with domain tools (periodic table, knowledge base, short/long-term memory) and operated in a closed-loop with a crystal structure generator and a property predictor, then it will achieve higher success rates and V.U.N. scores in generating chemically valid, unique, and novel inorganic materials with target formation energies than tool-free LLMs or diffusion-only baselines because tool grounding and iterative feedback direct the search toward compositions and structures matching the desired energy constraints.
